concerns.text: Task T028 ('Implement selectivity target extraction... from reactant symmetry') lacks a concrete algorithmic definition. The spec defines the target as 'Regioisomer Diversity Count' derived from 'product SMILES metadata' (FR-003), while the plan defines it as 'reactant symmetry'. The task does not specify which logic to implement (product parsing vs. reactant symmetry calculation) nor the specific algorithm (e.g., 'calculate graph automorphism orbits on the reactant aromatic ring'). Without this, an implementer cannot deterministically execute the task.

Task T029 ('Implement Ordinal Logistic Regression') is unexecutable because it contradicts the spec's FR-004 which mandates 'Poisson Regression'. The task does not provide the necessary logic to handle this contradiction (e.g., 'Override FR-004 per Plan.md Section X'). An implementer cannot execute a task that violates a functional requirement without explicit instruction on how to resolve the conflict.

Task T030 ('Implement Random Forest regression') is mapped to FR-004 which mandates 'Poisson Regression and Random Forest'. While RF is present, the task description does not specify the target variable handling (deterministic count vs. stochastic count) or the specific library parameters required to match the 'deterministic theoretical value' logic defined in the plan. The task assumes context not present in the task description itself.

Task T032 ('Implement degenerate target detection... and switch to Binary Classification') references a fallback model (Binary Classification) that is not defined in the spec's FR-007 (which lists 'Binary Classification or Zero-Inflated Poisson'). The task does not specify the exact logic for the 'Binary Classification' switch (e.g., 'threshold > 1') or the specific implementation of the fallback, making it impossible to execute deterministically without guessing the implementation details.

Task T041 ('Documentation updates in docs/') is too coarse. It does not name the specific files to update (e.g., 'Update docs/reports/symmetry_analysis.md with invariance proof') or the specific content to add. The downstream atomizer will need to split this.

Task T042 ('Code cleanup and refactoring') is too coarse and vague. It does not specify which files, what kind of cleanup (e.g., 'Remove unused imports in code/ingestion.py'), or the criteria for completion. This is a classic 'do work' task without a concrete deliverable.

Task T043 ('Performance optimization across all stories') is too coarse. It does not specify the target metric (e.g., 'Reduce descriptor calculation time to < 10 mins') or the specific optimization technique (e.g., 'Vectorize SMILES parsing in code/ingestion.py'). The implementer cannot execute this without further decomposition.
